slides.title: Screen Time Before Bed
slides.body: Why it makes bedtime harder — and what to do instead.

slides.title: Screens Keep Kids Awake
slides.body: Blue light delays melatonin. Even 30 minutes before bed pushes sleep back and makes settling harder.

slides.title: Set a Screen-Off Time
slides.body: Pick a fixed time — same every night. When kids know it's coming, they stop negotiating.

slides.title: Build a Wind-Down Routine
slides.body: Bath, pajamas, teeth. The same order every night signals the brain that sleep is coming.

slides.title: Replace Screens With Calm
slides.body: Reading, drawing, quiet music. Give kids something that fills the same need — without the stimulation.

slides.title: Consistency Does the Work
slides.body: After a few weeks, most kids stop fighting bedtime entirely. The routine becomes what evenings simply look like.

caption:
Screen time right before bed is making bedtime harder than it needs to be 😴

Here's what's actually happening — and a simple routine that fixes it 👇

Save this for tonight.
